text: PASSAGE A: All over the world till lately... mankind has been breeding up to the maximum... The author observes that
options: war, pestilence and famine were caused by the extravagance of nature
there was a time when uncontrolled birth made sense
It was wise at a time when mankind did not interfere
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Uncontrolled reproduction made sense in an earlier period when deaths from war and famine were also high.

text: PASSAGE A: Which of these statements does not express the opinion of the author?
options: Mankind has started to interfere with the work of nature
Many people had died in the past through want and disease
Mankind should not have the maximum number of children possible
Mankind should take care of its children
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The author focuses on population control, not specifically child care.

text: PASSAGE A: 'Humane' as used in the passage means
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Humane' here means kind/compassionate versus nature's 'heartless' play.

text: PASSAGE A: 'We must consciously try to establish an equilibrium' implies that mankind must
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Equilibrium means balance. Mankind must purposely balance birth and death rates.

text: PASSAGE A: The main idea of this passage is that
options: man should control the birth rate
mankind will soon perish of starvation
pestilence causes more death than war
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The core argument is managing the birth rate alongside the reduced death rate.

text: PASSAGE B: When may a journalist have to act against his own inclination?
options: When he becomes weak and decrepit
When his enemies make a mistake
When his political opponents frame misleading headlines
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Honesty requires giving credit to opponents when they perform well.

text: PASSAGE B: A journalist would be able to uphold the tenets of his profession if he
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Fairness and resisting bias are the pillars of the profession.

text: PASSAGE B: 'Insidious' means
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Insidious means sneaky or subtle in a harmful way.

text: PASSAGE B: A dutiful journalist must
options: be able to recognize merit wherever found
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Professionalism means acknowledging excellence even in rivals.

text: PASSAGE B: The duty of the journalist to the public entails
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The ultimate duty is recording and reporting the truth.

text: PASSAGE C: 'Frame of reference' means
explanation: Sabi Explanation: It refers to the specific circumstances surrounding a situation.

text: PASSAGE C: The writer believes through science and technology our country can achieve
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Technology is linked to material and economic well-being.

text: PASSAGE C: Basic considerations for science should be: 1. know-how 2. raw materials 3. pollution 4. tradition 5. population 6. capital
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Refers to raw materials, technical know-how, and capital.

text: PASSAGE C: The main idea of this passage is that
options: each nation must plan its development according to its needs
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Nations must develop science policies based on their own context.

text: PASSAGE C: The term 'our sister developing countries' implies
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Refers to other developing nations like those in Africa.

text: The students were asked to ____ their assignments before the deadline.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Use the base verb 'submit' after 'to'.

text: Neither the teacher nor the students ____ present at the meeting.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: With 'neither...nor', verb agrees with the closer subject (students).

text: He has been absent ____ Monday.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Since' marks a specific point in time.

text: The boy was accused ____ stealing the money.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: You are 'accused OF' something.

text: Choose interpretation: He kicked the bucket.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Kick the bucket' is an idiom for dying.

text: She spilled the beans.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Spill the beans' means to reveal a secret.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Accommodation: 2 'c's, 2 'm's.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Environment: Don't forget the 'n' before 'ment'.

text: The principal, together with the teachers, ____ present.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Together with' doesn't change the singular subject (principal).

text: Interpretation: She is under the weather.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Under the weather' means feeling ill.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Maintain -> Maintenance (it changes!).

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Pronounce -> Pronunciation (the 'o' drops out).

text: The committee ____ yet to arrive.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Committee' is a collective noun acting as a single unit.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Embarrass: 2 'r's, 2 's's.

text: In the match against the uplanders team, the sub mariners turned out to be the dark horse.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A 'dark horse' is someone or a team that succeeds unexpectedly.

text: Only the small fry get punished for such social misdemeanors.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Small fry' refers to people who are not important or powerful.

text: He spoke with his heart in his mouth.
options: with a lot of confusion in his speech
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The idiom means he was very nervous or frightened while speaking.

text: The leader in today's issue of our popular newspaper focuses on inflation.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A newspaper 'leader' is the editorial article presenting the paper's opinion.

text: From the ways my friend talks, you can see he is such a bore.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A bore is someone who is dull or uninteresting.

text: His jail terms were to run concurrently.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Concurrent' means happening at the same time.

text: There is some obvious symmetry in the whole presentation.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Symmetry suggests balance and neat arrangement, meaning orderliness.

text: The bill has to wait as we are now insolvent.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Insolvent means unable to pay debts, which is bankrupt.

text: All his plans fell through.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To fall through means to fail to happen or succeed.

text: The balance sheet at the end of the business year shows that we broke even.
options: had no money to continue business
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Breaking even means income and expenses are equal. There is no profit and no loss.

text: He was appointed specifically to put the recruits through.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Put through' here means to train or drill the recruits.

text: The result of his experiment represents a breakthrough in medical science.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A breakthrough is a major success or important advance.

text: Being an optimist, our professor always sees the bright side of most things.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The 'bright side' is the positive or hopeful side of things.

text: The state government appointed a commission of inquiry to go into the community's complaints carefully.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To 'go into' complaints carefully means to investigate them.

text: A man lost a very lucrative job because he was held up by traffic.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To be held up means to be delayed.

text: Mrs. Dada has been deserted by her husband because he feels she has a heart of stone.
options: she has little warmth of feeling
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A heart of stone means someone is cold and lacks feeling.

text: Mr. Jack could be a successful businessman if he paid more attention to the more intricate aspects of his account.
options: Jack will have a very good chance of succeeding
Mr. Jack will find it difficult to succeed
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The sentence suggests success is possible if he pays attention, so he has a good chance.

text: If my father had not arrived, I would have starved.
options: My father did arrive and I didn't starve
I had to starve because my father didn't come
My father didn't arrive and I didn't starve
I should have starved but I didn't
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This is a third conditional. It means the father did arrive, so starvation did not happen.

text: The two sprinters were running neck and neck.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Neck and neck' means exactly even or level with each other in a race.

text: He stared at her.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To stare is to look fixedly and steadily. 'Fixedly' best captures that intensity.

text: When the man was caught by police, he presented a bold front.
options: he walked up to the policemen
he faced the situation with apparent boldness
he bravely attempted to give them a present
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To present a bold front means to appear brave even if one is afraid inside.

text: I have been able to observe him at close quarters.
options: within a short space of time
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'At close quarters' means from very near by, or at close range.

text: At the site of the ghastly accident the poor woman's hair stood on end.
options: the wind blew her hair upwards
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The idiom 'hair stood on end' means she was extremely frightened or horrified.

text: When the beggar was tired, he .... down by the roadside.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The correct past tense of 'lie' meaning to rest is 'lay'.

text: He did not like .... leaving the class early.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: After the verb 'like', the object pronoun 'us' is needed.

text: She is one of the best players who ____ in the team.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Players who' is plural, so the verb must be plural ('play').

text: Choose the word nearest in meaning: The manager was very ANGRY about the report.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Furious' is a stronger synonym for 'angry'.

text: The witness gave a VIVID account of the incident.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Vivid' means producing powerful, clear, and detailed images in the mind.

text: The teacher COMMENDED the student for his performance.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To commend someone is to praise them formally.

text: His excuse was not PLAUSIBLE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: If something is 'not plausible,' it is unconvincing or hard to believe.

text: The man is known for his GENEROSITY.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Generosity is the quality of being kind and generous.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The water is SHALLOW.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of a shallow surface is a deep one.

text: The man is very LAZY.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Active' or 'industrious' is the opposite of lazy.

text: The answer is CORRECT.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The direct opposite of correct is wrong.

text: He is very POLITE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of being polite (having good manners) is being rude.

text: The building is STRONG.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of strong is weak.

text: Interpretation: He is on cloud nine.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To be on 'cloud nine' is an idiom meaning to be extremely happy.

text: They buried the hatchet.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To 'bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

text: She has a heart of stone.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A 'heart of stone' implies someone is cold, cruel, or unfeeling.

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: It’s 'definitely'—remember there is 'finite' in the middle.

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Occurred' requires a double 'c' and a double 'r'.

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A common trick! It’s 'separate' with an 'a' in the middle.

text: Each of the boys ____ given a prize.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: 'Each' is a singular distributor, so it takes the singular verb 'was'.

text: She insisted ____ going there alone.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The verb 'insist' always takes the preposition 'on'.

text: He is fond ____ music.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The adjective 'fond' is followed by 'of'.

text: We arrived ____ the airport early.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Use 'at' for specific points/buildings and 'in' for cities/countries.

text: The boy was very TIMID.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Timid means showing a lack of courage or confidence; shy.

text: The man is quite ARROGANT.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Arrogant means having an exaggerated sense of one's own importance or abilities.

text: The instructions were OBSCURE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Obscure means not discovered or known about; uncertain and confusing.

text: She was RELUCTANT to speak.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Reluctant means unwilling and hesitant.

text: The teacher was ASTONISHED.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To be astonished is to be greatly surprised or amazed.

text: Opposite: The road is ROUGH.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of a rough surface is a smooth one.

text: The water is DIRTY.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The direct opposite of dirty is clean.

text: He is very WEAK.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of weak is strong.

text: The answer is WRONG.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of wrong is correct.

text: The room is EMPTY.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of empty is full.

text: Interpretation: He let the cat out of the bag.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This idiom means to reveal a secret accidentally.

text: He hit the nail on the head.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This means to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem.

text: They are in hot water.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To be 'in hot water' means to be in trouble or a difficult situation.

text: He is a chip off the old block.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This means someone who is very similar in character to one of their parents.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Don't forget the 'n' in the middle—government.

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: It's 'privilege'—no 'd' allowed!

text: Choose correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Remember the rule: 'i' before 'e' except after 'c'.

text: He would have passed the exam if he ____ harder.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This is a third conditional (past unreal). The structure is 'if + past perfect, would have + past participle'.

text: She is looking forward ____ her results.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The phrasal verb is 'look forward to', which is followed by a noun or gerund.

text: He is used ____ early.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: When 'used to' is preceded by 'be' or 'get', it is followed by the -ing form (gerund).

text: She prefers tea ____ coffee.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: In English, we always use 'to' with 'prefer' when comparing two things.

text: Choose the word nearest in meaning: The leader was very RESOLUTE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Resolute means admirably purposeful, determined, and unwavering.

text: The instructions were PRECISE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Precise means marked by exactness and accuracy of expression or detail; clear.

text: The boy is very NAIVE.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Naive means showing a lack of experience, wisdom, or judgment; innocent.

text: Her performance was OUTSTANDING.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Outstanding means exceptionally good; excellent.

text: The man is very STUBBORN.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: A stubborn person is determined not to change their attitude or position on something.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The room was TIDY.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of tidy (neat/ordered) is dirty or messy.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The boy is HONEST.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The direct opposite of honest is dishonest.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The story was INTERESTING.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Something that is not interesting is boring.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The man is RICH.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of rich is poor.

text: Choose the word opposite in meaning: The road is NARROW.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: The opposite of a narrow space is a wide one.

text: Interpretation: He is barking up the wrong tree.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This idiom means to be pursuing a mistaken line of thought or course of action.

text: Interpretation: She is in high spirits.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This means to be in a very good mood or to be exd.

text: Interpretation: He is pulling your leg.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This idiom means to deceive someone playfully; to tease or joke.

text: Interpretation: They are at loggerheads.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: To be 'at loggerheads' means to be in strong disagreement or dispute.

text: Interpretation: He is a man of his word.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: This describes someone who keeps their promises and can be trusted.

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Address: It needs double 'd' and double 's' (DD and SS).

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Occasion: It has a double 'c' but only a single 's'.

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Necessary: One collar (c), two sleeves (ss).

text: Choose the correct spelling.
explanation: Sabi Explanation: Committee: Double 'm', double 't', double 'e' (MM, TT, EE).
